Experiment 5: Reduced 1-4 RSA — Correlation Distance — Object Position

5-predictor regression (C1-C4 only): neural RDM ~ βA + βB + βC + βD + βE + error

Model: LLaMA-2-13B-Chat | Metric: Correlation | Position: object | 56 items × 4 conditions = 224 sentences | Permutations: 10,000 | FDR: Benjamini-Hochberg (q=0.05)

Table of Contents

  • 1. Stimulus Design
  • 2. Sample Stimulus
  • 3. Model Specification
  • 4. VIF Check & Model Correlations
  • 5. Model A: Beta & Unique Variance
  • 6. All Model Betas
  • 7. Full Results Table
  • 8. Model RDMs
  • 9. Neural RDMs at Peak Layers
  • 10. Model A: Neural RDM Detail

1. Stimulus Design

56 items × 4 conditions = 224 sentences. Subject fixed to "He" throughout. Conditions C5 (dis_action) and C6 (scr_action) are excluded from this reduced analysis.

CodeLabelTemplateExample
C1mental_stateHe [mental verb] the [object].He notices the crack.
C2dis_mental[Mental verb] the [object].Notice the crack.
C3scr_mentalThe [object] to [mental verb].The crack to notice.
C4actionHe [action verb] the [object].He fills the crack.

Condition Feature Matrix

FeatureC1C2C3C4
Has subject "He"✓✓
Mental verb present✓✓✓
Action verb present✓
Grammatical word order✓✓✓
Scrambled form✓
Object noun present✓✓✓✓
Mental verb + object (grammatical)✓✓
Action verb + object (grammatical)✓

7 verb categories × 8 items each: Attention, Memory, Sensation, Belief, Desire, Emotion, Intention.

2. Sample Stimulus

Item 1 (Attention): mental verb = notices, action verb = fills, object = the crack

ConditionSentence
C1 (mental_state)He notices the crack.
C2 (dis_mental)Notice the crack.
C3 (scr_mental)The crack to notice.
C4 (action)He fills the crack.

3. Model Specification

ModelNamePredicts similarity when both in...Purpose
AFull Attribution{C1}Target: bound subject + mental verb + object
BMental Verb + Object{C1, C2}Controls for grammatical mental-verb-object binding without subject
CMental Verb Presence{C1, C2, C3}Controls for mental vocabulary clustering
DVerb + Object{C1, C2, C4}Controls for grammatical verb+object binding regardless of verb type
ESubject + Verb + Object{C1, C4}Controls for having a subject with a grammatical verb+object frame
neural_RDM = βA + βB + βC + βD + βE + error

βA significant means: C1 pairs are more similar in the model's representations than predicted by any additive combination of sharing a mental-verb+object frame (B), mental vocabulary (C), a verb+object frame regardless of type (D), or a subject+verb+object frame regardless of verb type (E).

Reduced design rationale: Dropping C5 and C6 reduces the RDM from 336×336 to 224×224, increasing the proportion of C1×C1 pairs from 2.7% to 6.2%. This improves sensitivity to Model A while retaining the key contrasts needed to partial out confounds.

4. VIF Check & Model Correlations

Variance Inflation Factors

VIF < 5: OK. VIF 5–10: proceed with caution. VIF > 10: regression not trustworthy.

ModelVIFStatus
Model A: Full Attribution2.02OK
Model B: Mental Verb + Object4.57OK
Model C: Mental Verb Presence2.75OK
Model D: Verb + Object3.92OK
Model E: Subject + Verb + Object2.49OK

8. Model RDMs

5 binary model RDMs (224×224). Each predicts similarity (0) for sentence pairs where both belong to the specified condition set.

9. Neural RDMs at Peak Layers

Neural RDMs (correlation distance) at the most significant layer for each model predictor. Rows and columns are organized by condition.

10. Model A: Neural RDM Detail

Large neural RDM at Model A’s peak significant layer. Numbers in each block show the mean correlation distance for that condition pair. The C1×C1 block (upper-left) has the largest within-condition distance, confirming the negative βA: full mental state attributions individuate rather than cluster.

Interpretation: Negative βA means the model differentiates among specific mental state attributions (“He notices the crack” vs. “He believes the story”) more sharply than it differentiates among control conditions. Full attributions occupy a larger region of representational space — consistent with finer-grained compositional semantic encoding when subject, mental verb, and object are bound together.
